paper_title: A speculative model for carbon flow in diatoms during P-replete and P-limited conditions

methods.description: This method models the carbon flow in diatoms under P-replete and P-limited conditions, using P. tricornutum as a template. It describes the metabolic pathways and changes in carbon allocation during different nutrient conditions.

steps.description: Use P. tricornutum as a template for the model based on previous flux balance analysis of primary metabolites.
steps.searchable_summary: use P. tricornutum flux balance analysis as model template

steps.description: Model carbon flow under nutrient-replete, autotrophic growth conditions, focusing on high photosynthetic rate and CO2 assimilation by the Calvin cycle.
steps.searchable_summary: model carbon flow under nutrient-replete conditions
outputs: carbon flow model under nutrient-replete conditions

steps.description: Model carbon flow under P-limited conditions, highlighting changes in carbon allocation towards storage as TAG or excretion as ECPS.
steps.searchable_summary: model carbon flow under P-limited conditions
outputs: carbon flow model under P-limited conditions

steps.description: Simulate the degradation of phospholipids and replacement with sulfolipids, galactolipids, and betaine lipids as part of the P scavenging strategy.
Incorporate the inhibition of phosphofructokinase and activation of fructose bisphosphatase due to increased citrate levels, switching glycolysis towards gluconeogenesis.
Model the entry of G6P into the OPPP and its conversion to UDP-glucose for biosynthesis of various lipids and sugars.
steps.searchable_summary: model G6P entry into OPPP and conversion to UDP-glucose
